mcqs.question: What makes a class abstract in C++?

options.text: It contains at least one pure virtual function
It has only private member functions
It inherits from multiple base classes
It has no data members

mcqs.explanation: A class becomes abstract when it contains at least one pure virtual function, declared with = 0 syntax.

mcqs.question: What happens if you try to instantiate an abstract class?

options.text: Runtime error
Compilation error
The program runs but objects behave unexpectedly
It creates an object with default implementations

mcqs.explanation: The compiler will generate an error because abstract classes cannot be instantiated due to their incomplete nature.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is the correct syntax for a pure virtual function?

options.text: virtual void func() {} = 0;
void virtual func() = 0;
virtual void func() = 0;
pure virtual void func();

mcqs.explanation: The correct syntax is virtual returnType functionName(parameters) = 0; without braces or implementation.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of a virtual destructor in an abstract base class?

options.text: To make the class abstract
To ensure proper cleanup of derived class objects when deleted through base class pointers
To prevent instantiation of the class
To improve performance

mcqs.explanation: A virtual destructor ensures that when deleting objects through base class pointers, the correct derived class destructor is called, preventing memory leaks.

mcqs.question: Can an abstract class have constructor functions?

options.text: No, abstract classes cannot have constructors
Yes, but only default constructors
Yes, constructors are used when creating derived class objects
Yes, but only private constructors

mcqs.explanation: Abstract classes can have constructors which are called when derived class objects are created, typically to initialize base class data members.

mcqs.question: What happens if a derived class does not implement all pure virtual functions from its base class?

options.text: Compilation error
The program runs but with undefined behavior
The derived class also becomes abstract
The missing functions get default implementations

mcqs.explanation: If a derived class doesn't implement all pure virtual functions, it remains abstract and cannot be instantiated.

mcqs.question: Which of the following statements about abstract classes is FALSE?

options.text: They can contain data members
They can have non-virtual member functions
They can be instantiated if all pure virtual functions are defined
They can have virtual functions with implementations

mcqs.explanation: Abstract classes cannot be instantiated even if all pure virtual functions are later defined. The presence of = 0 in the base class makes it permanently abstract.

mcqs.question: How does an abstract class differ from a concrete class in terms of polymorphism?

options.text: Abstract classes cannot be used polymorphically
Only concrete classes can be used with base class pointers
Abstract classes define interfaces for polymorphic behavior
Concrete classes cannot override virtual functions

mcqs.explanation: Abstract classes define interfaces that enable polymorphism by requiring derived classes to implement specific behaviors that can be accessed through base class pointers/references.
